Hi Peter, I wanted to send a quick note ahead of the weekend.
As the only Democrat in the country who can flip a Governor's seat blue in 2025, I feel the pressure to _win big_ at the ballot box. Experts are calling this race a toss-up and our opponents are launching new attacks every day, so I'm asking my strongest supporters for a small contribution.
_Will you step up before you log off for the day?_
[_**Make a contribution of $25 or more today to my campaign for Governor. name: Eric Trump
subject: My brand new book just dropped
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Never Surrender, Inc
body:
Did you catch the news? Read it right now
| | | | [](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
---
Friend, I just announced my FIRST book, and I know you won’t want to miss this new look into the world of MAGA!!
[UNDER SIEGE](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
[](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
[READ IT NOW](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
My family has been under siege since my father came down the Trump Tower escalator. From raids on my childhood home at Mar-a-Lago, to assassination attempts and countless witch-hunt cases, the attacks didn’t just target my family, _**they targeted America.**_
But I wouldn’t trade it for anything. It’s all been worth it for MAGA and supporters like you!
I dive into these highs and lows, and share some never-released-before stories in my book. I discuss life in the Trump world, my time as head of a multibillion-dollar empire, share stories about **_The Celebrity Apprentice_** , and my time on the campaign trail. Read it now and better understand our movement!

[UNDER SIEGE](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
[READ IT NOW](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
Thank you, Eric Trump

[](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)

[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/114697022/567863456/-39258144?refcode=0808-e-ake-el-sl&sourceid=1056943&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MzA0Y2E3Yy1iNThhLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=689Al9lti9VOhKMOYNoDvJF6bjNZ7U7Vz9LhEF8LVaY=) Peter, the special election in TX-18 is fast approaching. We have just two months to elect Amanda Edwards to Congress. This is the critical stretch: Amanda's campaign is focused on the ground game, talking to voters, and making sure Texas families know what's at stake.

****[**Peter, will you split a donation between EMILYs List and Amanda Edwards today to help fuel this fight?**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/114697023/567863457/-39258144?refcode=0808-e-ake-el-sl&sourceid=1056943&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MzA0Y2E3Yy1iNThhLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=689Al9lti9VOhKMOYNoDvJF6bjNZ7U7Vz9LhEF8LVaY=)**** | [SPLIT A DONATION](https://click.everyaction.com/k/114697024/567863458/-39258144?refcode=0808-e-ake-el-sl&sourceid=1056943&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MzA0Y2E3Yy1iNThhLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=689Al9lti9VOhKMOYNoDvJF6bjNZ7U7Vz9LhEF8LVaY=)
---
Amanda leads with vision, experience, and heart. As a native Houstonian, former city councilwoman, and community advocate, Amanda brings the grit and grace this office deserves.

This race is about delivering a strong voice and leader for a community that has been without a voice in Congress for months, and Amanda's track record proves that she is prepared for the fight of today, while building a vision for tomorrow.

**🚂 Infrastructure & Transportation Leader: **On City Council's Transportation, Technology, and Infrastructure Committee, Amanda initiated and led the High Capacity Transit Task Force for the entire eight-county Houston region — expanding access to reliable, modern transit in one of America's fastest-growing cities.

**💰 Fiscal Accountability Champion:** As vice chair of the Budget & Fiscal Affairs Committee, Amanda authored measures to strengthen accountability, led oversight sessions on Houston's $5.1 billion budget, and pushed for smart stewardship of city resources.

**🤝 Disaster Relief Organizer:** After Hurricane Harvey dumped 51 inches of rain in 2017, Amanda mobilized hundreds of volunteers to aid Houston's most vulnerable. She focused relief efforts on low-income seniors, ensuring ongoing recovery for households too often left behind.

Amanda is the kind of leader Texas — and Congress — urgently needs.

****[**Together, we can elevate Houston's next generation of leadership — and show that Democratic pro-choice women in power means action. Split a donation between Amanda's campaign and EMILYs List today: **](https://click.everyaction.com/k/114697025/567863459/-39258144?refcode=0808-e-ake-el-sl&sourceid=1056943&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2MzA0Y2E3Yy1iNThhLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=689Al9lti9VOhKMOYNoDvJF6bjNZ7U7Vz9LhEF8LVaY=)****

clean_body: Support a leader that Texas — and Congress — urgently needs. | = Peter, the special election in TX-18 is fast approaching. We have just two months to elect Amanda Edwards to Congress. This is the critical stretch: Amanda's campaign is focused on the ground game, talking to voters, and making sure Texas families know what's at stake. **= | = Amanda leads with vision, experience, and heart. As a native Houstonian, former city councilwoman, and community advocate, Amanda brings the grit and grace this office deserves. This race is about delivering a strong voice and leader for a community that has been without a voice in Congress for months, and Amanda's track record proves that she is prepared for the fight of today, while building a vision for tomorrow. 🚂 Infrastructure & Transportation Leader: On City Council's Transportation, Technology, and Infrastructure Committee, Amanda initiated and led the High Capacity Transit Task Force for the entire eight-county Houston region — expanding access to reliable, modern transit in one of America's fastest-growing cities. 💰 Fiscal Accountability Champion: As vice chair of the Budget & Fiscal Affairs Committee, Amanda authored measures to strengthen accountability, led oversight sessions on Houston's $5.1 billion budget, and pushed for smart stewardship of city resources. 🤝 Disaster Relief Organizer: After Hurricane Harvey dumped 51 inches of rain in 2017, Amanda mobilized hundreds of volunteers to aid Houston's most vulnerable. She focused relief efforts on low-income seniors, ensuring ongoing recovery for households too often left behind. Amanda is the kind of leader Texas — and Congress — urgently needs. =** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you for standing with Amanda, and standing with progress. — the EMILYs List Campaigns team = | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you for being a supporter of EMILYs List. This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Inbox overcrowded? Sign up to get less email . We'd hate to see you go, but if you want to stop receiving all email, unsubscribe . Have a question? name: GOTV PAC
subject: Well Trump’s economy is here….
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GET OUT THE VOTE PAC NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR ANY CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email09042025&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email09042025&amount=25]
Peter ,
Today's jobs report was brutal: just 22,000 jobs added in August.
Unemployment is rising. Inflation is heating up. Job growth is crawling to a halt.
This is the Trump economy!
He promised working families better, but his reckless tariffs and chaotic policies are driving us straight into another downturn. Instead of taking responsibility, Trump is firing public servants, threatening the Fed, and looking for someone else to blame.
We can’t let Republicans spin this disaster into another con. If Democrats are going to take back the House in 2026, we need to hold Trump and his enablers accountable, and that means reaching voters now .
GOTV PAC is building the grassroots effort to turn economic frustration into electoral power by funding text and phone banks in the most competitive races and investing in candidates at the ground floor. But to keep up the fight, we need your help today.
Can you chip in to help us take this message to every voter who needs to hear it? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email09052025]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

Let’s show Trump and his cronies that the American people are done paying the price for their corruption and incompetence.
-GOTV PAC
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email09032025&amount=25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/gotvpacemq2?refcode=Email09032025&amount=25]
Get Out the Vote PAC mobilizes voters often overlooked by mainstream campaigns to help Democratic candidates win in the most competitive districts.
Our professional phone bankers specialize in long- and short-term GOTV campaigns, event registration, voting assistance in heavily gerrymandered states, fundraising, and call time management. Our mission is to flip red districts blue, any elected position from dog walker to Congress member.
Learn more at: gotv.vote [https://www.gotv.vote/]
PAID FOR BY GET OUT THE VOTE PAC NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR ANY CANDIDATE'S COMMITTEE
GOTV PAC
712 H St NE
PMB 96591
Washington, DC 20002
United States
Get Out the Vote PAC is registered with the Federal Election Commission as a hybrid Political Action Committee (PAC) with FEC ID C00839407. Our mission is to Flip the House by supporting targeted Congressional districts and down-ballot races within those districts. Our internal research evidences that the impact of a Dollar invested in this manner amplifies the top and bottom of the ticket.

name: David Hogg
subject: This is what helps me sleep at night
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
I take this job pretty seriously, Peter.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20250905-1&t=1&akid=2631%2E587540%2E5bizFZ)
Peter,
I take this job pretty seriously.
I spend a lot of time thinking about how to help every single one of the candidates we endorse win their elections. And you want to know one thing that helps me sleep at night?
It’s knowing that, since our launch in 2023, **more than 15,000 of our grassroots supporters** have signed up to make monthly recurring donations to Leaders We Deserve.
**[These donations are crucial to sustaining a movement like ours, so today I wanted to reach out and ask if you would set up a monthly recurring donation too.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250905_1_recurring?refcode=em20250905-2631&amount=5&recurring=1&refcode2=2631_587540_5bizFZ&akid=2631%2E587540%2E5bizFZ)**
_Use the links in this email to start a recurring donation to Leaders We Deserve._
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250905_1_recurring?amount=5&refcode=em20250905-2631&recurring=1&refcode2=2631_587540_5bizFZ&akid=2631%2E587540%2E5bizFZ)**
Here’s why these recurring donations are so helpful:
* They allow us to set a baseline for what we bring in each month, so we can budget that money toward our most pressing priorities.

* They help us get to work well before Election Day. A month before the election is too late to hire and train organizers, buy TV ads, and compete with more established, well-financed campaigns.

* They help create a snowball effect for our candidates. A lot of donors and organizations look at how much early money candidates raise to inform whether they should get involved, and recurring donations let us back young, progressive candidates early on to show their credibility.

**[So if you’re ready to elect more of the leaders we deserve, please sign up to make a monthly recurring donation of any amount that works for you today. It will help more than you know!](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250905_1_recurring?refcode=em20250905-2631&amount=5&recurring=1&refcode2=2631_587540_5bizFZ&akid=2631%2E587540%2E5bizFZ)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250905_1_recurring?amount=5&refcode=em20250905-2631&recurring=1&refcode2=2631_587540_5bizFZ&akid=2631%2E587540%2E5bizFZ)**
Thanks again for all your support,
David Hogg

P.S. Can’t commit to a monthly donation today? Not a problem. [**If you can make a one-time contribution of $5 or any amount at all, you’ll make a big difference in helping us reach our goals and elect the next generation of leaders.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_lwd_20250905_1_recurring?refcode=em20250905-2631&amount=5&refcode2=2631_587540_5bizFZ&akid=2631%2E587540%2E5bizFZ)
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20250905-1&t=2&refcode2=2631_587540_5bizFZ&akid=2631%2E587540%2E5bizFZ)**

name: Sen. Patty Murray
subject: RFK Jr. must be fired. Sign now if you agree.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY PEOPLE FOR PATTY MURRAY
body:
Tell RFK Jr.: 'You're FIRED.'
| | |
---
Peter,

As soon as Trump's former CDC Director revealed that RFK Jr. was pushing to fire her for refusing to put politics over public health and science, I was quick to call for his immediate firing that same night. This was a senior Trump official, nominated by the President, endorsed by RFK Jr. himself, and confirmed by the Senate Republican majority – I was hopeful this could be a real inflection point to force true accountability for RFK Jr.

I was glad to see many other Democrats join me in forcefully calling for RFK Jr. to be fired. Now, I'm asking you to **[join me in demanding RFK Jr. be FIRED from the Department of Health and Human Services' top command](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114697137/567875151/1926214349?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk545-su-nd&refcode=jtk545-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNjBhYTM5Yi1iODhhLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7r_UsFZDtfuJtN0r92i3aLa_H86PNY2tmoenLLu3SUU=&emci=21f8dc2d-b889-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=e60aa39b-b88a-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=13116610)** – but first, I want you to understand exactly how we got here:
**FIRST:** RFK Jr. fired scores of federal health workers, gutted vital public health programs, cancelled grants for lifesaving cures, replaced expert leaders with anti-science quacks, and championed legislation that will force millions to lose health insurance under Medicaid – _and that barely scratches the surface._
**THEN:** The CDC director he appointed was **forced out** of her job for sounding the alarm about RFK Jr.'s personal mission to dismantle our public health infrastructure and put American lives at risk.
**SO THIS WEEK: After witnessing RFK Jr. lie shamelessly to my Senate colleagues during a committee hearing, I reiterated my call from a week earlier demanding that RFK Jr. be fired. And it’s not just Democrats, there are 1,000 current and former HHS staff demanding his resignation – or, his immediate termination. **
We cannot let RFK Jr. burn what’s left of our health agencies to the ground. The destruction he's already done will take decades to recover from – but the lives needlessly endangered by his dangerous policies are irreplaceable. **Now is an important moment to keep pushing.**
So until my GOP colleagues who now regret confirming him join me in calling for an end to RFK Jr.’s tenure, I've launched an emergency petition to push President Trump to tell him:_'You're __FIRED.'_
But if our pro-public health coalition can’t build a SWELL of grassroots support, our voices will be drowned out by the next new shocking distraction tactics to come out of the White House.
[**Peter, I need** at least 20,000 signatures before midnight**to prove there is overwhelming support for the immediate dismissal of RFK Jr. as health secretary, because lives are on the line – so will you please add your voice alongside mine right now?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114697138/567875171/1926214349?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk545-su-nd&refcode=jtk545-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNjBhYTM5Yi1iODhhLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7r_UsFZDtfuJtN0r92i3aLa_H86PNY2tmoenLLu3SUU=&emci=21f8dc2d-b889-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=e60aa39b-b88a-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=13116610)
| | [Sign Now to Tell RFK Jr.: _YOU'RE FIRED_ >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/114697139/567875174/1926214349?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&ask=5&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&code=jtk545-su-nd&refcode=jtk545-su-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDM4NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNjBhYTM5Yi1iODhhLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=7r_UsFZDtfuJtN0r92i3aLa_H86PNY2tmoenLLu3SUU=&emci=21f8dc2d-b889-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=e60aa39b-b88a-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=13116610)

Patty

name: Rashida Tlaib via Justice Democrats
subject: They’re trying to censure me for speaking out about Palestine.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

A war-mongering Republican Congressmember is trying to bully me into
silence by introducing a new measure to censure me for speaking up against
the genocide of the Palestinian people.

I’m being targeted for my speech at last weekend’s People’s Conference for
Palestine in my home city of Detroit—where I called out the majority of my
colleagues in Congress who keep voting for genocide while ignoring the
overwhelming majority of Americans who are demanding an arms embargo.

They can censure me, but they can’t censure everyone who’s working toward
Palestinian liberation. During August’s congressional recess, people
across the country came to town halls to demand their representatives stop
funding and arming genocide.

Our movement is growing, and we won’t let war-mongers silence us or stop
our efforts to save lives. The more they try to suppress us, the more we
need to get louder and more courageous.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250905-3-rt?refcode=em20250905-36998&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36998_1203867_S5i9Wo&akid=36998%2E1203867%2ES5i9Wo ]Will you split $5, $15, or more between Justice Democrats
and my campaign to show that we won’t be silenced and we aren’t going
anywhere?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250905-3-rt?refcode=em20250905-36998&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36998_1203867_S5i9Wo&akid=36998%2E1203867%2ES5i9Wo ]PUSH BACK AGAINST CENSURE

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250905-3-rt?refcode=em20250905-36998&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36998_1203867_S5i9Wo&akid=36998%2E1203867%2ES5i9Wo ]A picture of Rashida Tlaib standing at a podium with microphones. There
is a sign on the podium that reads 'People's Conference for Palestine;
Gaza is the Compass; Detroit; August 2025'.

At the People’s Conference for Palestine last weekend, I welcomed people
to the city that raised me and spoke about how the U.S. government doesn’t
value human life, in our country or abroad.

I explained that as a daughter of Detroit, it’s no surprise to me that our
government—which is complicit in war crimes including enabling forced
starvation and supplying bombs that are dropped on Gaza’s hospitals and
schools—is also defunding Americans’ food assistance and healthcare while
turning its military forces on people in U.S. cities.

Detroit, the biggest majority-Black city in the country, has shown me how
to fight for civil rights and justice. My neighbors have experienced U.S.
troops on our streets, and they’ve risen up and birthed movements.
Detroit’s community mothers have taught me that our political institutions
won’t save us or free our people: We will do that. Social change comes
from organizing in our communities and pushing these institutions to do
what is right.

Our movement is working: More Democrats in Congress are swearing off AIPAC
funding, and more Democrats are supporting policies to restrict U.S.
military aid to Israel’s government.

But most elected leaders are still deeply out of touch with their
constituents. For example, last week the Democratic National Committee
voted down a resolution for an arms embargo—even though a poll came out
beforehand showing that 75 percent of Democratic voters and 60 percent of
all U.S. voters oppose sending more military aid to Israel.^1

This recent Quinnipiac University poll also found that 77 percent of
Democrats and half of all U.S. voters say Israel is committing genocide
against Palestinians in Gaza. I will keep pushing our government to stand
with the American people and stop the Israeli government’s genocide of
Palestinians.

I will continue defending human rights at home and abroad, including our
constitutional right to protest against genocide. Recently, I went to the
University of Michigan to join student activists and workers who are being
punished by the public university for their pro-Palestine advocacy.
Dissent is critical in a democracy—and academic freedom is part of it.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250905-3-rt?refcode=em20250905-36998&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36998_1203867_S5i9Wo&akid=36998%2E1203867%2ES5i9Wo ]Can you push back against attempts to silence me and our movement, and
stand up for our right to dissent, by donating to my campaign and Justice
Democrats today?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250905-3-rt?refcode=em20250905-36998&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36998_1203867_S5i9Wo&akid=36998%2E1203867%2ES5i9Wo ]Contribute $5

Thank you so much. Together we will keep fighting for our rights and for
the safety and dignity of all people.

With you in the fight,

Rashida

1 - [ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140951?t=1002&akid=36998%2E1203867%2ES5i9Wo ]Majority Of Voters Oppose Deploying National Guard To D.C.,
Quinnipiac University National Poll Finds; Support Drops For U.S. Military
Aid To Israel As 50% Think Israel Is Committing Genocide In Gaza

They can censure me, but they can’t censure everyone who’s working toward Palestinian liberation. During August’s congressional recess, people across the country came to town halls to demand their representatives stop funding and arming genocide. Our movement is growing, and we won’t let war-mongers silence us or stop our efforts to save lives. The more they try to suppress us, the more we need to get louder and more courageous. [ ]Will you split $5, $15, or more between Justice Democrats and my campaign to show that we won’t be silenced and we aren’t going anywhere? [ ]PUSH BACK AGAINST CENSURE [ ]A picture of Rashida Tlaib standing at a podium with microphones. There is a sign on the podium that reads 'People's Conference for Palestine; Gaza is the Compass; Detroit; August 2025'. At the People’s Conference for Palestine last weekend, I welcomed people to the city that raised me and spoke about how the U.S. government doesn’t value human life, in our country or abroad. I explained that as a daughter of Detroit, it’s no surprise to me that our government—which is complicit in war crimes including enabling forced starvation and supplying bombs that are dropped on Gaza’s hospitals and schools—is also defunding Americans’ food assistance and healthcare while turning its military forces on people in U.S. cities. Detroit, the biggest majority-Black city in the country, has shown me how to fight for civil rights and justice. My neighbors have experienced U.S. troops on our streets, and they’ve risen up and birthed movements. Detroit’s community mothers have taught me that our political institutions won’t save us or free our people: We will do that. Social change comes from organizing in our communities and pushing these institutions to do what is right. Our movement is working: More Democrats in Congress are swearing off AIPAC funding, and more Democrats are supporting policies to restrict U.S. military aid to Israel’s government. But most elected leaders are still deeply out of touch with their constituents. For example, last week the Democratic National Committee voted down a resolution for an arms embargo—even though a poll came out beforehand showing that 75 percent of Democratic voters and 60 percent of all U.S. voters oppose sending more military aid to Israel.^1 This recent Quinnipiac University poll also found that 77 percent of Democrats and half of all U.S. voters say Israel is committing genocide against Palestinians in Gaza. I will keep pushing our government to stand with the American people and stop the Israeli government’s genocide of Palestinians. I will continue defending human rights at home and abroad, including our constitutional right to protest against genocide. Recently, I went to the University of Michigan to join student activists and workers who are being punished by the public university for their pro-Palestine advocacy. Dissent is critical in a democracy—and academic freedom is part of it. [ ]Can you push back against attempts to silence me and our movement, and stand up for our right to dissent, by donating to my campaign and Justice Democrats today? [ ]Contribute $5 Thank you so much. Together we will keep fighting for our rights and for the safety and dignity of all people. With you in the fight, Rashida 1 - [ ]Majority Of Voters Oppose Deploying National Guard To D.C., Quinnipiac University National Poll Finds; Support Drops For U.S. Military Aid To Israel As 50% Think Israel Is Committing Genocide In Gaza Contribute to Justice Democrats:

name: Brianna For Colorado
subject: A Special Session & September Sprint 🏃‍♀️
body:
| | | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/36281b9ae55d/a-special-session-september-sprint?e=8249791cac)
---
| | | | [|
---](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=a34bf1f7ad&e=8249791cac)
| | | | | | | | | | | | Hello friends and happy September! I’m sorry for not getting a newsletter out in August, our office was extremely busy with Colorado’s special session. That means we have a lot to talk about in this newsletter so let’s get into it!
This month’s edition will discuss the recent Special Session, my new interns, upcoming constituent and [community](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=04bb466cc2&e=8249791cac) events, and in case you missed it headlines. But first, let’s talk about some stuff I have been up to!
---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| |
---
|
---
| | I started off August by being invited to speak at PERIOD and IGNITE’s “Activate Your Advocacy Bootcamp”. This bootcamp was to help young people learn the basics and importance of getting civically engaged. I had a wonderful time and was honored to speak.
---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| |
---
|
---
| | I visited the 41st Denver Rustlers celebration, an event that helps support young ranchers and farmers of Colorado.
---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| | | I went to Elevate Quantum’s “Quantum COmmons Open House”. This event celebrated the grand opening of the first major building on the Quantum Commons in Arvada. Congrats to Elevate Quantum on their success!
---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| |
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| | Finally, I attended the Colorado Boulder County Democrat’s “Big Bill Fallout Emergency Town Hall” where constituents got to hear from legislators the impact that H.R. 1 will have on the state.
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| |
## [SPECIAL SESSION]()
---
|
---
| | The special session began on August 21st and ended on August 26th. The reason Colorado had a special session was due to Republicans in Congress blowing a $1 billion hole in the state budget. Legislators came to the special session ready to responsibly address this crisis with a balanced approach to close corporate tax loopholes, establish a framework to cut spending where possible, and use some of the state’s rainy-day savings to protect the core services people rely on.
---
| | New laws passed this special session closed corporate tax loopholes and preserved access to services:
* [HB25B-1001 Qualified Business Income Deduction Add-Back](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=92d37ae893&e=8249791cac)
* [HB25B-1002 Corporate Income Tax Foreign Jurisdictions](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=f2c964cf29&e=8249791cac)
* [HB25B-1003 Insurance Premium Tax Rate for Home Offices](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=e52348d21d&e=8249791cac)
* [HB25B-1004 Sale of Tax Credits](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=26f4435dd2&e=8249791cac)
* [HB25B-1005 Eliminate State Sales Tax Vendor Fee](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=2b4d20d56c&e=8249791cac)
* [HB25B-1006 Improve Affordability Private Health Insurance](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=3453b0ece0&e=8249791cac)
* [SB25B-001 Processes to Reduce Spending During Shortfall](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=e00d183679&e=8249791cac)
* [SB25B-002 State-Only Funding for Certain Entities](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=99fe0e8419&e=8249791cac)
* [SB25B-003 Healthy School Meals for All](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=7751512c23&e=8249791cac)

---
| | Another focus of the special session was on my previous AI bill [SB24-205](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=391f33df10&e=8249791cac). I worked to further this prior legislation and attempted to do so as a prime sponsor of [SB25B-004](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=a9719127fa&e=8249791cac). However, I excused myself as prime sponsor due to disagreements over where the legislation landed due to keeping consistent with my previous votes. Regardless, I plan to continue to expand my current AI regulation work in the upcoming 2026 regular session by continuing the work we did in the special session.
---
| | If you would like to more about the special session, read these articles:
* [Here’s why the Colorado legislature is now meeting in a special session — and what’s at stake](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=eaa9d617de&e=8249791cac)
* [Colorado lawmakers return to Capitol to fix budget as they tangle over Trump tax bill — and solutions](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=9c0e0e81fc&e=8249791cac)
* [Colorado’s legislature has filled a third of budget gap by slashing tax breaks. Here’s what comes next](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=7210c203d2&e=8249791cac)
* [Another artificial intelligence deal collapses — as Colorado lawmakers’ budget fixes near finish line](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=211fe9b46b&e=8249791cac)
* [Colorado’s AI regulations split lawmakers as they pitch reforms for special session: ‘It’s a mess’](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=25270558e6&e=8249791cac)
* [Colorado lawmakers hit a familiar wall — Big Tech’s influence — in effort to rewrite AI regulations](https://briannaforcolorado.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=e7333941d4549e2ed73aa08fb&id=20ca3c8672&e=8249791cac)

---
|
---
| |
## [INTERNS]()
---
|
---
| | I am pleased to introduce my 2026 Legislative Interns, Dylan Blue and Nicole Ostermiller!
---
|
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| |
---
|
---
| | Dylan is from New Jersey with a Bachelor of Science in Psychology. He is currently entering his first year at the University of Denver to receive a Master of Social Work. His policy interests are in housing equity, technology, and the economy.
---
| | | | | | | | | | |
---
| |
---
| | Nicole is from Utah, also with a Bachelor of Science in Psychology. They are currently their first year at the University of Denver to receive a Master of Social Work and a Master of Public Policy. Nicole’s policy interests are in LGBTQ+ rights, housing access, and economic equity.
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| | I am so happy to welcome Dylan and Nicole to my office. They have both already been such a tremendous help during special session and I cannot wait to see what the regular session brings!
---
|
---
| |

clean_body: | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Hello friends and happy September! I’m sorry for not getting a newsletter out in August, our office was extremely busy with Colorado’s special session. That means we have a lot to talk about in this newsletter so let’s get into it! This month’s edition will discuss the recent Special Session, my new interns, upcoming constituent and events, and in case you missed it headlines. But first, let’s talk about some stuff I have been up to! | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | I started off August by being invited to speak at PERIOD and IGNITE’s “Activate Your Advocacy Bootcamp”. This bootcamp was to help young people learn the basics and importance of getting civically engaged. I had a wonderful time and was honored to speak. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | I visited the 41st Denver Rustlers celebration, an event that helps support young ranchers and farmers of Colorado. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | I went to Elevate Quantum’s “Quantum COmmons Open House”. This event celebrated the grand opening of the first major building on the Quantum Commons in Arvada. Congrats to Elevate Quantum on their success! | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Finally, I attended the Colorado Boulder County Democrat’s “Big Bill Fallout Emergency Town Hall” where constituents got to hear from legislators the impact that H.R. 1 will have on the state. | | | | | [SPECIAL SESSION]() | | | The special session began on August 21st and ended on August 26th. The reason Colorado had a special session was due to Republicans in Congress blowing a $1 billion hole in the state budget. Legislators came to the special session ready to responsibly address this crisis with a balanced approach to close corporate tax loopholes, establish a framework to cut spending where possible, and use some of the state’s rainy-day savings to protect the core services people rely on. | | New laws passed this special session closed corporate tax loopholes and preserved access to services: | | Another focus of the special session was on my previous AI bill . I worked to further this prior legislation and attempted to do so as a prime sponsor of . However, I excused myself as prime sponsor due to disagreements over where the legislation landed due to keeping consistent with my previous votes. Regardless, I plan to continue to expand my current AI regulation work in the upcoming 2026 regular session by continuing the work we did in the special session. | | If you would like to more about the special session, read these articles: | | | [INTERNS]() | | | I am pleased to introduce my 2026 Legislative Interns, Dylan Blue and Nicole Ostermiller! | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Dylan is from New Jersey with a Bachelor of Science in Psychology. He is currently entering his first year at the University of Denver to receive a Master of Social Work. His policy interests are in housing equity, technology, and the economy. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Nicole is from Utah, also with a Bachelor of Science in Psychology. They are currently their first year at the University of Denver to receive a Master of Social Work and a Master of Public Policy. Nicole’s policy interests are in LGBTQ+ rights, housing access, and economic equity. | | | | | I am so happy to welcome Dylan and Nicole to my office. They have both already been such a tremendous help during special session and I cannot wait to see what the regular session brings! | | | [UPCOMING CONSTITUENT EVENTS]() | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Capitol Coffee Talk ☕️ SEPTEMBER 11TH @ 8:00 am Let’s discuss your policy ideas while enjoying some of the best coffee in town! 📍ZIGGI’S COFFEE | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Beers with Brianna 🍺 SEPTEMBER 28TH @ 7:00 pm Join me to share your views and chat about the legislative session, all while enjoying a local craft brew! 📍MOUNTAIN TOAD BREWING | | | | | [Virtual Office Hours]() 💻 | | We have a simple sign-up process to schedule some one-on-one time with Rep. Titone at YOUR convenience, either via Zoom or phone. Calendly! | | | Can't make it to any of my scheduled events? Don't hesitate to reach out if you have something on your mind or need help finding local resources. I'm just an email or phone call away! mailto:brianna.titone.house@coleg.gov?subject=&body= | cell: 720.213.6660 | office: 303.866.2962 | | [ IN THE DISTRICT]() | | ARVADA | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | I am deviating a little bit from Arvada events this month to give a giant shoutout to the Arvada Fire Department for putting out the . Thank you for keeping our community safe and for all of the hard work that you do! | | | | | To find events coming up in Arvada, check out ! | | | GOLDEN | | COMMUNITY CLEAN-UP DAYS | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | September 13th & 14th Bring trash, electronics, and scrap metal for recycling from 8:00 a.m.–3 p.m. on September 13 & 14 to 1300 Catamount Drive. Electronics with cords and items at least 50% metal are accepted. Proof of Golden residency required (don't forget your ID with a City of Golden address). This is a great opportunity to responsibly recycle and keep Golden looking its best. | | | | For a list of upcoming Golden events, check out the the City’s. | | [ ICYMI!]() | | | BRIANNA IN THE NEWS The Colorado Sun: The Colorado Sun: The Colorado Sun: CPR News: Colorado Newsline: Colorado Newsline: The Sum & Substance: Denver 7: Colorado AI News: | | STATE & LOCAL NEWS Denver Post: Post: Colorado Newsline: Denver 7: Denver 7: | | NATIONAL STORIES NPR: Associated Press: | | GOOD NEWS 🎉 | | | | FIND ME ON SOCIAL MEDIA! Follow me to stay up to date with news, photos, bill details, events, community resources, and much more! 📲 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | The legislative session is over, but my work for you doesn’t stop. This summer, in addition to researching my next round of bills, I’ll be starting my campaign forstate treasurer**! If you like the work I do, please consider a donation to my campaign or host a fundraiser in your community. | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Representative Brianna Titone (she/her/hers) Chair, Joint Technology CommitteeVice Chair, Finance CommitteeAppropriations CommitteePension Review CommissionChair, LGBTQ Caucus mailto:brianna.titone.house@coleg.gov?subject=&body=303.866.2962 office | | | | | | | | | | A Message From Representative Brianna Titone, 2025 Want to change how you receive these emails? | | **[There’s an old story in Scripture.__Maybe you know it.__](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/CjfcaPvULrEzy6q9qhRGZb2nRYF3IA_-F7BePM_9E2pg6s0KmKUPJKFZLVTgDpjY28xFxtRUEmKnkTz_0twi-yYJW35svjnZllxYxDXNeOBjpxiba3ELUKQ8hYMacSnxReI-_iFRv6soOoFbwDf0iw9i5g3wJTidzT2ueG0vd7ed7fKqCspPI5OqRP2XXKOWFZUapg_4Z0pklbga1U7rkPDZpk9QO4loEASsJEoOnDVSJ2vCpFRW4BgRbscSDAce8orX1byPPlr2UvToz320WOrNU8cfjnPCKkKGnrL30h0lJr8BX4Kgmo3ZiSG8MkwQI6XES5YDaSaWVLpYVCDmbuqXNzKqgIvXaOyANpZXKKoje8-eP_Q5T6I93c2R4lgbk-f8__OixrnXXiTF6uR9j7T_IY5u9pETnOKfQklMEd9TlbxpatOxRjVweZGzeJ1tr1b5rTN8fe_nWA91U_0MxGTheJKa-G8R-NhhV59QU7HhXEHo22lRZXrAPg2hB46rp2C39gLFin7N5i6tvSXldPDYf9QUFGtegALTQGuTEH39MFTC9mufZhPbCUcisrNYk_OGUBhy0Nxua_njoe4yX-Lj21-vFpAPzyP1arMDuTeKmsOF_Dyv4McDKup-5XmMMGw03kW1iTP6CtavRMM67QE0m6SySUj5ic7u63C4QlqxNGOdd8JPjv4uKuuNGDuUbKtCg5K3zN43gmJKu__l7Y8FtdgLkyHhJWJ6IgmXhqhz-g0jSesmCYfV9SdRLAzUplgOmbHFaiKO5zC0nX0c6DJkWUD9CXG6roSlG0PLcpsSRSnmhMT5JuzwRiS3fJbP9S_QFcw9NrSOCh9QLdO_4tvjJdXmd2WMfm-L49Bih05V9pq9LQ/N6LJwFv1wZFZBmgoRoxj8_-cjLrFZIqm/12)__** A broken woman. Alone. Ashamed. Cast out. She came to the well at noon, hoping **no one** would see her. But Jesus _**_did_**_ see her. He didn’t shame her. He didn’t cancel her. [**He offered her living water.**](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/w0tRv81bJMVpGWvIKzFR1wjO8XKQITOnnnUjQU0dWmqINrcYT57LMQZvAhoHmI4i14Az23q2IPoW9uV0E10xsoYQ_oGNSMWdqs63zH-1H6xbnVDofSvHlDfsp229SSXitrckbMg-z9mae7tTFHJJp0aqWjPjQ00I3gUOpuiwt2qymhzesesjmku8TLP4Z45-PYfY7bvsSqTnlCdEBH1__e0GGhLmiJ77bk6yt5qPWMUG45u40hRXZGFg2UJ5i4PB3SoOwylXWTc7tTQFJwoDjNa30p-tLIl0UswtOvhS89THwkgS44L-GDnz9C3mq1VXZBVjOiP4-g0EHxd4ZTVF453rCm8jZrKgCmQIwYPmqYZD_GT0vNE6qdUPSSBoxR3xEDP3GXpi4bv84ReFsqvxstwIoGcoyW7hT3uzHZLEQZYWQfyQNT07VckqFh0NdbqrnOBPvEeo7I-20RcP_-cQ8mC0OFT6d0gS5FDth397aswUuZZ4ycw56C3cYp6uCcSUTLFjbrT8m4R7PH9e4uZMrV2bhoXGLa_LLBeAecEGJnSHaLy7QXFn4I13QcuWvVNpIw6hFyKHOwak2He9wj0d-VpjFBeB1a2CwDkkJhf3OrKDJKflrzWHav5Q5L_ebCqVPnoLPzbwBCCn6KCSbZiML-i2apx9ShQWNgHfiQ6jLO1PHVJ0GtnoQHy-_qcCucqdNXoZ7wARQJPBW4ZUMJoIyB-Q2cvRcBfmd3a-Hq-LklX04_PZy12OHGUC6e6TsVfKC63FfBu5Q-lSBCAkyuFPQEzXuX9JQZ-EJEL-R3QeJd0UDYbZTHl5ZshJLvCvVKzXZQbkeWZm8SSPk5LH_JbntxOoEQLvF6XSX-Gocs_5Qvs9POAntw/pmmEgwzZ4S2fcMji0iFjL1ckq0zxFLQ7/12) She was stunned. Jesus said: **[“If you knew who I was, you would’ve asked me for a drink. And I would’ve given you living water.”](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/fIwNyJcPJDmcjhYeCC3f5mCROZcbFIrCOoRTOWaFhBvnOWSJlKWq86K3RzB7dMiyjm91_0B0nEOt3j_ixMdtXIifeO51zKsgtpycwab-4fE_ww59twkctUx8SsPqZSW3dLzcREaR9bajb-A6HlR1_13zU40VWDi3D89FHklY3XrYDEbQTJulzserzXIBG2W3scRvcUw46LxXqX2Nfa_V4BlxM9p9pmFdZZEutTuRa-Yi1J--mETnMTgmKnuraHAaDZcBbtGyrQo9sbx6SPTrGOINPNPVPM3OHVhHX0ToVGe_Cdh1ngsZ0ZyPHoxF03AoLg9-wo9riYVel3zb_z83BYcdNSAIquFgg31tCMkP3k3o5OxC4ca3TR5l3tBvqYjuL5HnOJkwhBsbV7DWCqR9up-jg8Wpw7ecG6KOqaxRPf5QytH1W9oJZslJkHRo5Gh8hS9YTK0fNcRuSfKyDzHbzTrtOaRX1WIrtV9Oui-L06ngInJaF1JOLNmNch7DeGy5wN6NPw2cKik2HglXg12-Ut-eMq0HsdqXQoluRinUzPz7nM2HcAwvAJD1Ktc5nG03qG3NbpUBFhMAeQ-8ScMBTIES_hud8NLTmD5282eVDCUjysDW2rSu3lAoOrAp85Ob_mUVucctzLQbPMflKnTRK7tlZgPbBJ4taYMvxlq8CPZoAx834F676ygK3h1d43qTn6YqAmrmCdO7ZxAFam7qObF9I2vV0ED9gRXRtD-nxR15X0iTOyL70bHmJY1jAR9eNZM0t0g-KAh-LwoDlLDAOahoJAU254G7dqn8vm_mPPFW9AXxQ36crKf948Zo2BeSot7gpXVb5ad6MeOBq2sTyR-pIb-IOfDridEdoknWmI-E6Z2brA/6nhRG-5ULGolVGp8y2JveRgb9EOFtlgu/12)** That story hits me deep— __**because I was that woman.**__ I’ve been told I wasn’t good enough. **[But God doesn’t need perfection; He uses the broken to carry out His perfect plan.](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/KTZL379YLAJvtWU1k-FSii6fKoVSa8-8s5cridUXeAmOyMfFCzTCwkRTrwucNUjIGg_7mWibMSXljy_h89sMHnX36PgDtuab1geNAhLfM5LyH6w4FuiQfLo2Ltt5RJGItfAT5G6Y76kn3Oa6OCiwKsmIGm6ylElB-6W2QgFzv_zuqc2HgZAQqb2mTlpGG4vJwpJXWdsXD3wo8BavNPJ8qBTmuPB7ojnPS7jQVSyVn9Dg1rPLVHwv8iPpSatO5-0ecRBPPklV4teZ5n6r_wkU3QFARN7aJlV4jJmx3mJGAtacnYO2haDWtwmWVcDST-Ep0OmyjjOxmAgHnTwRJhHQm0x59NPubA95uOeOQa_3HojTFMGpPGMxfzn5EIB6qyYA8LmvIDtVLD5QFO2iPEyY3_UDRfxS7n_b8V-EcUfaMdFxGrgMY6VdbJT2RJ5FAqeinMVYuRKeJYBz5m0Q348Wk2JFKfTd-5cSTzxbU-ry7LKnoB9u6wknenEObqiDmH1XMMc-xCyZSb6lDyFPSeU0LE0Ma1tkb29h3983zuXTewesAhgy-LdazTdYbV8y5hykiBhzwV8XUso11ubtYnGC-TsBgU28PXjTcJkFkVhP4gXM9zCOl0ZZSou11nimktFM4WsG923WglW_VsRgKF76gRt7aanlzX8pE0vElG8mMVs5RR0_9wkiQ2zHOu_3QmFCvUjEeFLHA-twX_cTkSx_PQIW11xqkU__QVE8rK6f-BRldxRoXHfsrlw3vf7IH4L93lNsxakgWw5JXyRoIjDDzAVqlxHwz2U6HWHSB0_RERdd37DvA6RsWuk_QrPLUrqT_QF2kPeSaJ1hOKoREaZU09i4h31e7PuH1rmhu4g3VyEpbkxoqw/LuyLq0eB4HNj3uGHuOaxQ6SKPkYMIHzV/12)** At 17, I dropped out of high school. My parents gave me two choices: **_Go back to school, or get a job._** **[So I got to work...at Waffle House. Graveyard shifts. Spatula in hand.](https://links.iterable.com/s/c/--rPWwDoQZQ-OWZ3yALjupZxwYqRmzG_d5AI-4_qe38iiXayk_V6j2a_FLEupmtkLolt6qlVAXld7Fk1Iz6AEyhHwlnWp-mLJy8hpMQfn7txSJyLLNLf-LH1b3mOCuTh77M8UbqhjgFLvOEnr1PTFgRLOexTJvabjyWWsmlaBNgWaiVeQ38ELCyKMiTmfuRI8sF6BIz1_4-HZfKM6PBkc2ag3-A0nhgx4XnAJ0iGkmX8k_TeYP2PiIKx9XdhxwzEMB4tdWuyOpbaqMV5JISvCLnmDeHAP1VLYUfqaYCj9XksSKAdSMvi3Txf417odtMjFNSNWE83Co5odncolp6cHXaR1l0zEiYsFgKbeTE7Slq65clVBtmpGf9Pn3BIH-PGck8sa-fhVyr7bjs0uWMyOCZwJvcZfGwDYUijB62qMH2NhLsgDvF4loV8uVNQPbR6TB1BpYDWijNsTnrZpVzlqqGYGiwohzAXLJqAOo_tfI1_r6-0-2YE0SdPsLTzByZqraTUEjOZZtJqcg6JR3eWyr8WpMpznWftKXn7jBQTH9U_8lyLFkVrsnK3mNyoAhtEakNyQusVksq9gRDh3q29cb4P0_63a0exh9j41gcBJPlrf1YC8jXupwiRwwv_8Q0exx8BcdlpnQcB8RRRplGPOUKznE7b7pR_-BzD03YalRVmARKUNJvvZC1mAcAXbpQX8UMncEqZXqegyyoSYifnDaOMqbAVZ1nvBEzfzXWadnFMRSh6KhFoFBkXiZZz8sE0zNM46QU45asc6BkleBsPMwhN2zuaHbxNc8uSyicxvUzMEEKLT9wagXMa4X40J-4pflKMibn9gQuHSWR8nVi97ZWILfZlEpi8m9VKvmVy5e5JLBBTjQ/Vm2yO_67bNYkxFs0YFReziIdIgPyMT5z/12)** It was humbling. It was hard. _**But it wasn’t the end of my story.**_ 25 years later, that high school dropout became the **first woman to graduate from The Citadel, and the first Republican woman elected to Congress from South Carolina.**

**High school dropout. Waffle House waitress. First woman to graduate from The Citadel. Single mom. Fighter.** **Nancy Mace wasn’t born into power. She worked for every inch of ground she’s gained.** At 17, she dropped out of high school and took a job slinging hash browns at Waffle House to survive. No silver spoon. No Ivy League connections. Just grit, grace, and a whole lot of graveyard shifts. **By the grace of God, she turned that struggle into a story no politician can touch.** She made history as the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, South Carolina’s legendary military college. She built her own business. She raised two kids on her own. And when the Left came for everything she believed in, she stood her ground.Now, she’s running for Governor of South Carolina. Nancy Mace is a battle-tested conservative who’s walked through fire and come out swinging. In Congress, she’s been one of President Trump’s fiercest allies, fighting to secure our border, protect our daughters, and stop the Left’s sick agenda dead in its tracks. **She’s not here to make friends. She’s here to make history.** Nancy is fighting to:
* Eliminate South Carolina’s Income Tax—the highest in the Southeast
* Prosecute violent criminals and pedophiles with zero leniency
* Crack down on sanctuary sheriffs who let illegal aliens roam free
* Defund radical public universities that indoctrinate, not educate
* Reform education so students learn how to think—not what to think
* Work hand-in-hand with President Trump to put South Carolina FIRST

Through every hardship, Nancy never gave up. And now she’s ready to do what she’s always done: roll up her sleeves, take the heat, and lead from the front. **South Carolina is worth fighting for. And Nancy Mace is just getting started.**
---
| |

She was stunned. Jesus said: That story hits me deep— because I was that woman. I’ve been told I wasn’t good enough. At 17, I dropped out of high school. My parents gave me two choices: Go back to school, or get a job. It was humbling. It was hard. But it wasn’t the end of my story. 25 years later, that high school dropout became the first woman to graduate from The Citadel, and the first Republican woman elected to Congress from South Carolina. And I didn’t do it alone— God carried me. He carried me through trauma, heartbreak, single motherhood, public failure, and private grief. He carried me when I was assaulted. When I was raped. When I was diagnosed with PTSD. When I moved four times to keep my children safe. When the world called me unworthy, God called me redeemed. When the Left tried to silence me, God gave me a voice. When I had nothing left to give, God filled me with living water. I know what it means to be broken. And I know what it means to be rebuilt by grace. A fight for our churches , our daughters , our freedom —and our souls. And I won’t sit back while spineless politicians sell out our country for fame and favor. Brandon Creighton's switch from lawmaking to Texas Tech leader signals a new era for higher ed," **Texas Tribune's **Jessica Priest** \-- "As the clock wound down on the year's [second overtime legislative session](https://substack.com/redirect/81890d9b-02df-44b6-afcf-245c1c256239?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), Lt. Gov.**Dan Patrick** , the powerful leader of the Texas Senate, took a few minutes to say goodbye to his right-hand man, Sen.**Brandon Creighton** : The architect of a recent bevy of laws reshaping higher education in Texas -- from the ban on diversity, equity and inclusion programs to limits on protests and faculty's influence on campuses -- was stepping down to take a new job overseeing those very changes.

Texas Tech regents voted Thursday to name the Conroe Republican their [sole finalist for chancellor](https://substack.com/redirect/4f68a9c0-8bb6-40d8-a35d-dae54ce6575b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the university system's top job, betting that his political connections and influence at the Capitol will help secure funding, expand research opportunities and elevate the system's profile.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/9a7b68a0-e8a5-462c-8db6-69fcaa492c50?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Chancellors are at the helm of each university system's fleet of campuses, and Texas universities have increasingly turned to politicians to lead them, a shift that Patrick celebrated Wednesday. He pointed to the Texas A&M and University of Texas systems' new chancellors -- **Glenn Hegar** , the state's former state comptroller and a former state senator, and former state Rep. **John Zerwas** , respectively -- as models of the kind of political leadership he believes universities need.

"There's a whole new look at least in Texas of who [university leaders] think should lead universities, people with legislative experience, people who have been accountable to the people, people who have a record," Patrick said. "You don't just have to have nine letters behind your name and some education degree to run a university when it really takes more than just that."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/9a7b68a0-e8a5-462c-8db6-69fcaa492c50?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Experts say regents have increasingly prioritized political savvy over academic leadership when picking university system leaders, a statewide and national shift they warn could make higher education less independent and give politicians greater influence over academics, research and campus life.

**Barrett Taylor** , a higher education governance expert at the University of North Texas, said Creighton's selection signals that Texas is entering a new phase in that transformation.

"Enacting laws was part of the agenda for reshaping public higher education in Texas, but it's not where it ends," he said. "There are going to be additional steps, and this is probably one of those steps."

**Ren ee Cross**, senior executive director at the University of Houston's Hobby School of Public Affairs, agreed.

"It just makes too much sense for public universities -- and for those in power -- to have, for lack of a better word, their 'lieutenants' in these high-ranking positions," she said. "If Lt. Gov. Patrick really wants to change the system of higher education in Texas -- and he's been pretty open about wanting to -- of course you'd want people you know and trust running these large university systems. I don't see that changing anytime soon. Even if Democrats controlled the state years from now, I could see them doing the same thing."

In an interview with The Texas Tribune, Texas Tech Board Chair **Cody Campbell** said regents believe the chancellor's job is fundamentally different from a campus president's, and should emphasize representation before state leaders rather than academic oversight.

"We do not need a chancellor who knows how to run the math department," he said." [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/6feb957d-58f5-43fd-823d-1523e1ce74fb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

**" Houston man charged with murder in shooting of 11-year-old boy after door knocking prank," **via AP**** \-- "Police have charged a Houston man with murder in [the fatal shooting of an 11-year-old boy](https://substack.com/redirect/f73eada4-3c2f-4dda-830d-a37a37f8ba82?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who knocked on the door of a home and ran away as a prank, police said Tuesday.

The 42-year-old man, identified by authorities as **Gonzalo Leon Jr**., was taken into custody and booked into the Harris County Jail in Houston early Tuesday. On local property records, Leon matches the name of the owner of the home where police say the boy knocked on the door.

Court records did not list an attorney for Leon to comment on the allegations.

The boy, **Julian Guzman** , and a cousin had been attending a birthday party Saturday night when they "became bored and wanted to ring doorbells, or play 'ding dong ditch,'" according to a probable cause affidavit. The prank commonly called "ding dong ditching" involves ringing a doorbell or knocking on a door and fleeing before someone inside opens the door.

Police departments around the country have issued public service announcements in recent months warning people that such actions aren't funny but dangerous. Homeowners have no way of knowing it's "just a prank," according to a June [Facebook post](https://substack.com/redirect/95a9c20c-2f62-4403-8536-cbca377da8d5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by the Georgetown, Kentucky Police Department.

Guzman's cousin told investigators he and Guzman knocked several times on Leon's door and ran away. In an initial statement, police had said Guzman rang a doorbell, but the affidavit said the boy knocked.

The final time he and the cousin knocked, Leon came out holding a pistol that he fired once into the ground. Leon then raised the pistol and fired at Guzman and his cousin, according to the affidavit.

"Our witness says the suspect came out of the door, ran out into the street and was firing down the road," Houston police Sgt. **Michael Cass** told reporters on Sunday.

Guzman's cousin told police that Guzman "cried out in pain that he had been shot," according to the affidavit. As Guzman's cousin was trying to drag the boy away, Leon slowly walked back to his house.

Guzman was shot in the back and died Sunday, police said.

"In my opinion, it doesn't look like any type of self-defense. It wasn't close to the house," Cass said.

Police found about 20 firearms in Leon's home, including AR-style rifles, shotguns and medium caliber pistols.

Texas and other states have some version of a "castle doctrine," either by law or court precedent, that says residents don't have to retreat when threatened in their homes but instead can respond with physical force. While Texas law gives people broad latitude to protect themselves, protect others or protect their property, there has to be a reasonable belief that force is immediately necessary in the situation.

**Seth Kretzer** , an attorney in Houston not connected to the case, said if the shooting happened as police allege then Leon would not have a strong case for self-defense under state law.

"You just can't shoot a kid in the street dead because he knocks on your doorbell and declare you felt threatened by him. I mean it's a little hard to believe a grown male with a gun felt threatened by an unarmed 11-year-old child running away on the street," Kretzer said." [AP](https://substack.com/redirect/498dd91e-3353-4e50-95ce-561c37691dd8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

**Alex Fairly guest column: "GOP purity tests are self-destructive," **AP's **Sean Murphy** \-- "Texas Republicans are enjoying what we in the business world refer to as Run Rate Acceleration. The 2025 Legislative session, while not perfect, was one of the most conservative in Texas history. Our governor, lieutenant governor and speaker of the House are working effectively together, as well as with their respective caucuses, to advance conservative legislation. And Speaker **Dustin Burrows** recently received the total endorsement of President **Donald Trump**. There is more to do, but Texas is unquestionably the nation's conservative beacon.

But certain factions within our party are working to undermine, attack and politically cancel some conservative legislators simply because they don't align 100% with the definition of conservative held by a narrow faction of the party. In business-speak, we call that counterproductive.

At a recent meeting of the State Republican Executive Committee, this faction pushed an overzealous effort to conduct a "performance review" that would have formally undermined a majority of Texas House Republicans via Rule 44 of the party's regulations. That effort ended with a more measured review being adopted. But members did adjourn the meeting by encouraging Republican precinct members across Texas to look for broader opportunities to censure candidates.

Censure is an important tool in party governance. But when it is overused, weaponized or used as a misguided purity test, it will weaken our movement, diminish our credibility and risk undoing what we have fought to build. That is why [I was willing to fund litigation](https://substack.com/redirect/de3f3c78-61df-4e5a-ace3-71617abbe66e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) opposing this measure. While we must uphold the principles we believe in, history shows that attempts to purify a movement almost always end in disaster, division and decline.

The counterexample is President Trump. He has dramatically expanded the Republican Party, bringing in millions of voters who only a few years ago would have never considered voting Republican. A recent[ analysis of voter registration data](https://substack.com/redirect/1baa5549-22fa-44d0-9d68-7e7fbc493bcc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by _The New York Times_ found that since 2020, Democrats have lost more than 2.1 million voters across 30 states and the District of Columbia, while Republicans have gained 2.4 million. Trump even appointed two former Democrat presidential candidates to his Cabinet.

Texas Republicans should passionately debate our differences, policies and priorities. We should each zealously support candidates who best reflect our version of conservatism. But we must not fall into the trap of insisting all Republicans fit into the single, narrow mold of a specific faction of our party. We must recognize that a conservative representing suburban Dallas may not look, sound or vote exactly like a conservative representative from Midland or Amarillo.

If we attempt to force all members into a certain mold, we will lose Republican seats and weaken our party. While we can never abandon our conservative principles for the sake of growth, our strength comes not from extreme conformity, but from the universal appeal of those principles and their ability to improve the lives of Texans.

A year ago I was in a camp that believed we must fight to rid ourselves of Republicans who aren't as conservative as I am. Today, I am no less conservative, but I have come to see the reality that things like Rule 44 and the recent fixation on censure are symptoms of a deeper illness in Texas conservative politics: the eagerness to attack and destroy Republicans who aren't exactly like us.

Republicans are in the middle of a historic surge. Voters are running toward our tent. This is a time for growth, strength and unity -- not division, confusion or infighting.

Imagine how much more effective we could be if we stopped fighting with one another and focused all our energy on fighting for every Texan.

_Alex Fairly is an Amarillo businessman and founder of the Texas Republican Leadership Fund PAC."_[DMN](https://substack.com/redirect/13ddd04e-1eb2-458c-ac39-f6e23bba5e48?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

**" Company behind East Texas water grab hired key lobbyists just before bill delaying it died," **Houston Chronicle's **Megan Kimble** \-- "The Texas Senate voted against delaying a [controversial East Texas groundwater export project](https://substack.com/redirect/d03922bf-0d5b-4faf-b66d-687fce634a23?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) on the same day the company behind it hired one of Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick's** top advisors as a lobbyist.

Conservation Equity Management, a company affiliated with Dallas investor **Kyle Bass** , hired**Allen Blakemore** on Tuesday, the same day the legislation hit the Senate floor, according to [lobbying records filed at the Texas Ethics Commission](https://substack.com/redirect/6139d758-f4ef-46aa-9824-2a1ef71396ca?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Blakemore is [Patrick's political consultant](https://substack.com/redirect/7c6ce465-eed5-412c-ba90-a97d371b75bd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and has also worked on the campaigns of several Republican senators.

The Senate, which Patrick oversees, ultimately agreed to back a state-led study of the company's proposal to pump huge amounts of groundwater out from under Anderson and Henderson counties. But it stripped a provision that would have put the project on hold until the findings came out in 2027.

The change "gutted the most important part of this bill," according to the author, state Rep. **Cody Harris** , whose district includes the counties Bass is looking to tap. The legislation [ultimately died on Wednesday night when the special session ended](https://substack.com/redirect/d8575c44-4610-4a28-9432-83207236fb92?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), in a win for Bass.

Blakemore directed questions to Bass, whose spokesperson did not respond to a request for comment. Patrick said in a statement that he was "not involved in it at any level."

He said the senator who sponsored the bill and the one who chaired the committee that vetted it made all the decisions.

"I told them we would do whatever they decided - it was up to them," Patrick said.

Two companies affiliated with Bass have applied for exploratory permits for wells capable of pumping more groundwater out from under the two counties than is currently available.

The companies have not yet applied for permits to export the water, but Bass signaled that is his intention, saying that the water was needed to address looming shortages elsewhere and could go "anywhere south of Waco and... north of Dallas-Fort Worth." The permit decision now rests with the State Office of Administrative Hearings.

Harris, a Republican from Palestine, [filed House Bill 27 in response to local backlash](https://substack.com/redirect/bf3409a1-fde0-4730-9ff6-d96a2ac95476?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The bill would have required the Texas Water Development Board to study the effects of groundwater pumping in the aquifers below Anderson and Henderson counties and present it to state leaders. In the meantime, the groundwater conservation district would not be able to issue a new production or export permit for the region.

Water experts have said that diverting water from the area's Carrizo-Wilcox Aquifer could impact downstream users of surface water - like the City of Houston - because the aquifer helps replenish rivers and streams.

Shortly after HB 27 was filed, [Bass lashed out at Harris](https://substack.com/redirect/bf3409a1-fde0-4730-9ff6-d96a2ac95476?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and threatened to sue him in a since-deleted post on X. "Small time Texas politician convincing the Governor... to allow an East Texas groundwater permit application to be delayed two years creates an immediate Federal Takings case," he wrote in August. "See you in Federal Court @CodyHarrisTX."

While the legislation easily cleared the House, state Sen. **Robert Nichols** , a Republican from Jacksonville and the bill's sponsor in the Senate, said the moratorium was removed because it was the only way to get the legislation out of committee and through the upper chamber.

During a committee hearing on Tuesday, several Republicans pushed back against the proposed moratorium, saying it set a dangerous precedent.

"At this point, how do we say that we're going to put a moratorium in just those two counties or that groundwater district and not everywhere else in the state?" said state Sen. **Paul Bettencourt** , a Houston Republican.

Records show Blakemore is one of several lobbyists Bass' company brought on in the waning days of the special session.

Two employees of Blakemore Public Affairs are also listed as lobbyists for the company: **Avery Dishaw** , a former staffer for Bettencourt, and Casey Haney. **David Whitley** , the former Secretary of State and deputy chief of staff to Gov. **Greg Abbott** , also lists Conservation Equity Management as a client, as of August 27.

According to the ethics commission records, Blakemore would be paid between $56,000 and $111,000 for work between September 2 and December 31, 2025." [Hou Chron](https://substack.com/redirect/101c84d3-d10f-4f0f-82b7-a85775829b51?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)
